title: Cherry-Pick, Replay, Patch Equivalence, and Backports · Graph Lens: Gita Cherry Pick Set Order
summary: A security fix must be backported across three maintained lines where commits differ, one patch is already present under a new ID, and the original series includes a merge. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita cherry pick set order increment in multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.
Protect the old tip, inspect rewritten mapping, compare patch series and trees with range-diff, test changed behavior, and recover from abort or error.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Cherry Pick Set Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G57 is a theory in a disposable repository. one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice. The learner must predict minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target, run git cherry -v release security-fix, explain the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work, test this changed condition: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior, and preserve this recovery boundary: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Cherry Pick Set Order contract for the supplied data.

The learner must predict minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target, run git cherry -v release security-fix, explain the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work, test this changed condition: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior, and preserve this recovery boundary: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations. A defensible solution applies Gita Cherry Pick Set Order, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-cherry-pick-set-order — Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice
git cherry -v release security-fix
# expected: minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target
# observation: the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work
# changed-case: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations
# transfer-limit: patch equivalence is heuristic and each supported runtime needs changed-platform tests
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Cherry Pick Set Order evidence

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Cherry Pick Set Order into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-cherry-pick-set-order-dkk38w
# scenario-key: trace-dkk38w probe-w83kkd boundary-k38wdk owner-dkk38wx fault-dkk38wz
# before-ref: main=a11cedk candidate=b00b18w
# hypothesis: one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice
git cherry -v release security-fix
# expected: minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target
# observation: the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work
# changed-case: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior
# after-ref: main=a11cedk candidate=c0ffeew
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations
# transfer-limit: patch equivalence is heuristic and each supported runtime needs changed-platform tests
